name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 22.09.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 22.09.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As on 19 September, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1115.86 lakh ha as compared to 1100.42 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 101.8 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As on 19 September, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1115.86 lakh ha as compared to 1100.42 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 101.8 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperatures is  likely  to  be above normal in East India, Central India, many parts of NW India and many parts of NE India.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperatures is  likely  to  be above normal in East India, Central India, many parts of NW India and many parts of NE India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From June 01 -Sept 21 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 7% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)18%; (ii) Northwest India: 31%; (iii) Central India: 11%; and South Peninsula: 8%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From June 01 -Sept 21 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 7% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)18%; (ii) Northwest India: 31%; (iii) Central India: 11%; and South Peninsula: 8%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · A low pressure area is likely to form over North Bay of Bengal during next 24 hours.
texts.text: A low pressure area is likely to form over North Bay of Bengal during next 24 hours.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Another low pressure area  is  likely  to  form  over  eastcentral  &  adjoining  North  Bay  of Bengal around 25 th  September. Moving west-northwestwards, it is very likely to become a depression  over  northwest  and  adjoining  westcentral  Bay  of  Bengal  off  South  OdishaNorth  Andhra  Pradesh  coasts  around  26 th   September.  It  is  very  likely  to  cross  South Odisha-North Andhra Pradesh coasts around 27 th  September.
texts.text: Another low pressure area  is  likely  to  form  over  eastcentral  &  adjoining  North  Bay  of Bengal around 25 th  September. Moving west-northwestwards, it is very likely to become a depression  over  northwest  and  adjoining  westcentral  Bay  of  Bengal  off  South  OdishaNorth  Andhra  Pradesh  coasts  around  26 th   September.  It  is  very  likely  to  cross  South Odisha-North Andhra Pradesh coasts around 27 th  September.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Isolated Heavy rainfall likely over Odisha, Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, Vidarbha & Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam during 24 th  -27 th  September, 2025 and likely to continue over Northeast India during next 3-4 days.
texts.text: Isolated Heavy rainfall likely over Odisha, Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, Vidarbha & Coastal Andhra Pradesh & Yanam during 24 th  -27 th  September, 2025 and likely to continue over Northeast India during next 3-4 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall likely over Islands, Northeast and Eastern parts of  India  and  isolated  to  scattered  rainfall  likely  over  the  central,  west  and  South Peninsular India.
texts.text: Fairly widespread to widespread rainfall likely over Islands, Northeast and Eastern parts of  India  and  isolated  to  scattered  rainfall  likely  over  the  central,  west  and  South Peninsular India.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Dry weather likely over the most parts of the North West India.
texts.text: Dry weather likely over the most parts of the North West India.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.
texts.text: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  18 September  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 163.91 billion cubic meter, which is 90% of total live storage capacity (182.479 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 103.5 percent of last year's storage and 119.3 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  18 September  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 163.91 billion cubic meter, which is 90% of total live storage capacity (182.479 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 103.5 percent of last year's storage and 119.3 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: text
texts.orig: the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Paddy (Hispa)) in Assam. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Paddy (Hispa)) in Assam. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · During  the  week  (11  Sept -17  Sept  2025),  higher  rainfall  was  observed,  as  compared  to  Long Period Average (LPA) by 7%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June to 17 th Sept 2025) is higher than LPA by 8%.
texts.text: During  the  week  (11  Sept -17  Sept  2025),  higher  rainfall  was  observed,  as  compared  to  Long Period Average (LPA) by 7%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June to 17 th Sept 2025) is higher than LPA by 8%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall,  rainfall  activity  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  over  central  and  peninsular  India; normal  to  above  normal  over  most  parts  of  northwest  &  east  India;  below  normal  over northeast India during the week.
texts.text: Overall,  rainfall  activity  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  over  central  and  peninsular  India; normal  to  above  normal  over  most  parts  of  northwest  &  east  India;  below  normal  over northeast India during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Withdrawal of Southwest Monsoon 2025
texts.text: Withdrawal of Southwest Monsoon 2025

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The  line  of  withdrawal  of  southwest  monsoon  continues  to  pass  through  Bhatinda, Fatehabad, Pilani, Ajmer, Deesa, Bhuj.
texts.text: The  line  of  withdrawal  of  southwest  monsoon  continues  to  pass  through  Bhatinda, Fatehabad, Pilani, Ajmer, Deesa, Bhuj.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Conditions are becoming favourable for further withdrawal of southwest monsoon from some  more  parts  of  Gujarat,  Rajasthan,  Haryana  and  Punjab;  some  parts  of  Himachal Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir and Uttar Pradesh during next 24 hours.
texts.text: Conditions are becoming favourable for further withdrawal of southwest monsoon from some  more  parts  of  Gujarat,  Rajasthan,  Haryana  and  Punjab;  some  parts  of  Himachal Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir and Uttar Pradesh during next 24 hours.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (19 Sept -25 Sept 2025):
texts.text: Week 1 (19 Sept -25 Sept 2025):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, maximum temperatures are likely to be above normal by 2-4°C over most parts of central & east India, north Peninsular India and over Uttar Pradesh. These are very likely to be near normal or below normal over remaining parts of the country during the week.
texts.text: Overall, maximum temperatures are likely to be above normal by 2-4°C over most parts of central & east India, north Peninsular India and over Uttar Pradesh. These are very likely to be near normal or below normal over remaining parts of the country during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (25 Sept -01 Oct 2025):
texts.text: Week 2 (25 Sept -01 Oct 2025):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Maximum temperatures are likely to be near normal or below normal over most parts of the country except Western Himalayan Region and northeast India, where these are likely to be above normal by 2-4°C during the week.
texts.text: Maximum temperatures are likely to be near normal or below normal over most parts of the country except Western Himalayan Region and northeast India, where these are likely to be above normal by 2-4°C during the week.

texts.text: Soil Moisture Observation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soil moisture as on 16 Sept 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand,  West Bengal, major parts of Odisha, Haryana, Punjab, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P. and southern parts of Bihar.
texts.text: Soil moisture as on 16 Sept 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand,  West Bengal, major parts of Odisha, Haryana, Punjab, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P. and southern parts of Bihar.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · It was lower than average of past 9 years in pockets of northern parts of Bihar , in pockets of eastern part of UP, in pockets of western parts of Assam and in parts of Tamil Nadu.
texts.text: It was lower than average of past 9 years in pockets of northern parts of Bihar , in pockets of eastern part of UP, in pockets of western parts of Assam and in parts of Tamil Nadu.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5.   CROP SOWING STATUS FOR KHARIF SEASON
texts.text: 5.   CROP SOWING STATUS FOR KHARIF SEASON

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Kharif Crops - Area coverage
texts.text: Kharif Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Rice :  About  438.51  lakh  ha  (108.0%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Kharif  Rice  as compared to 430.06 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rice :  About  438.51  lakh  ha  (108.0%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Kharif  Rice  as compared to 430.06 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Maize :  About  94.95  lakh  ha  (120.3%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Maize  has  been reported as compared to 84.30 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Maize :  About  94.95  lakh  ha  (120.3%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Maize  has  been reported as compared to 84.30 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Moong : About 34.79 lakh ha (97.5% of Normal) area coverage under Moong as compared to 34.96 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Moong : About 34.79 lakh ha (97.5% of Normal) area coverage under Moong as compared to 34.96 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soybean : About 120.44 lakh ha (94.7% of Normal) area coverage under Soyabean has been reported  as  compared  to  126.26  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year. Soyabean area diverted in Maize in MP and Maharashtra.
texts.text: Soybean : About 120.44 lakh ha (94.7% of Normal) area coverage under Soyabean has been reported  as  compared  to  126.26  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year. Soyabean area diverted in Maize in MP and Maharashtra.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Sugarcane :  About  59.07  lakh  ha  area  coverage  under  Sugarcane  has  been  reported  as compared to 57.22 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Sugarcane :  About  59.07  lakh  ha  area  coverage  under  Sugarcane  has  been  reported  as compared to 57.22 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Cotton : About 109.90 lakh ha (84.9% of Normal) area coverage has been reported compared to  112.76  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year.  Cotton  area  diverted  in Paddy and Groundnut respectively in Punjab & Haryana and Gujarat.
texts.text: Cotton : About 109.90 lakh ha (84.9% of Normal) area coverage has been reported compared to  112.76  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year.  Cotton  area  diverted  in Paddy and Groundnut respectively in Punjab & Haryana and Gujarat.

texts.text: Availability  of  fertilizer  has  been  found  comfortable.  Details  of  Requirement,  Receipt, Availability, Sales and Stock position of Major Fertilizers (Urea, DAP, MOP, Complex and SSP) during Kharif 2025 as on 19.09.2025 is given below:
